name: Grains Information- Whole Wheat
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine 1 part whole wheat with 2 parts water and ½-1 tsp salt.
2. Cook over high heat until boiling for 5 minutes.
3. Remove from heat, cover, and place in a preheated oven set to 300°F (150°C).
4. Turn off the oven and leave the wheat undisturbed overnight or for 8-10 hours.
5. Alternatively, use the Thermos method:
- Preheat a 1-quart thermos with boiling water.
- Boil 1 cup whole wheat with 2 cups water for 3 minutes.
- Place in the thermos, seal, and let stand overnight.
6. Grind or blend cracked wheat for cereal, casseroles, bread, cookies, etc., using a ratio of 1 part cracked wheat to 4 parts water.
7. Cook enough whole wheat to last at least a week; store airtight in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.
8. Reheat cooked whole wheat and serve with mushroom sauce or gravy instead of potatoes.
9. Use in place of rice for stroganoff, etc., and add to casseroles, chili, spaghetti sauce, sloppy joes, soups, stews, salads, sandwich spreads, etc.
Split Peas:
- Combine with other protein sources for good nutrition.
- Good in soups or as a vegetable.
- For quick soup: grind peas (whole or split) into flour and simmer with water (1 part pea flour to 3-4 parts liquid) for 3 minutes.
- Add raw vegetables to the blender or soup, and ham flavoring is optional.
Soybeans:
- Important in storage programs due to high protein content and mineral richness.
- Rich in B vitamins, unsaturated fats, and minerals.
- Can be grown at home and used green or dried; store dry soybeans in a cool, dry place and rotate every 3-5 years.
- To cook: soak overnight (3-4 cups water to 1 cup beans) in the refrigerator. If hard, boil for 2 minutes before soaking. Boil for 2-5 minutes, remove from heat, cover, and let stand for 1-2 hours. Use the soaking liquid if not bitter; simmer until tender.
- Soybeans can replace lima or navy beans in chili recipes, bean salads, etc.
